title: Chathanar identified as grain merchant
claim_or_observation: The author is identified as Kulavanikan Chathanar rendered in the supplied translation as a grain merchant
notes: Verify exact Tamil compound and commercial meaning

title: Occupational streets and specialist workers
claim_or_observation: The city includes shell and conch workers pearl-garland makers dancers grain sellers weavers gold valuers diamond merchants mahouts horse trainers officials and others
notes: Determine which city and exact occupational terms in Tamil

title: Performance requires multidisciplinary training
claim_or_observation: The curriculum of trained performing women includes dance music instruments languages bodily technique calculation painting flowers design and time knowledge
notes: Literary catalogue requires line-level Tamil verification

title: Merchant groups undertake sea voyages
claim_or_observation: Merchants travel in groups by ship and face provisioning abandonment storm and return-navigation risks
notes: Distinguish owners passengers and professional crew

title: Valuable wreck goods circulate
claim_or_observation: Sandalwood jewels and soft cloth are associated with sailors and shipwreck recovery
notes: Not a complete cargo manifest or ownership rule

title: Household alms enter public redistribution
claim_or_observation: Food moves from Athirai's household through Manimekalai toward wider feeding and relief
notes: Exact food and ritual terminology require Tamil review

title: Injury can destroy livelihood and force begging
claim_or_observation: Sudhamathi's injured father loses support and seeks food revealing economic vulnerability after bodily harm
notes: Do not generalise one narrative into a complete welfare system

title: Kodumanal craft and exchange node
claim_or_observation: Inscribed pottery beads carnelian iron objects and route context support specialised craft production and inland exchange
notes: Full excavation and technology reports required

title: Karur was an early historic trade centre
claim_or_observation: Tamil-Brahmi sherds amphorae rouletted ware coins stones brick and iron finds support inland commercial importance
notes: Imported objects do not identify every user or merchant

title: Alagankulam participated in coastal exchange
claim_or_observation: Mediterranean-region pottery rouletted ware amphora fragments Tamil-Brahmi sherds beads bricks and coins indicate coastal circulation
notes: Do not stage a Roman colonial settlement

title: Arikamedu bead production required specialised operations
source_work: Peter Francis Jr and later scholarship
claim_or_observation: Glass and stone bead manufacture involved multiple technical stages and long-distance distribution
notes: Chronology and production scale require specialist review

title: Lapidary workers formed connected communities of practice
claim_or_observation: Bead technologies across sites are better understood as varied connected practices than fixed ethnic industries
notes: Supports anti-ethnic-essentialism rule

title: Tamil-Brahmi marked portable economic objects
claim_or_observation: Names and marks occur on pottery rings seals coins and other portable objects across early historic Tamilakam
notes: A mark does not automatically identify maker owner or contents

title: Early corporate merchant reference is limited
claim_or_observation: A stray Tamil-Brahmi reference to Vellarai Nigamaththor suggests early corporate or merchant identity
notes: Meaning chronology and institutional scope require epigraphic review

title: Later guild systems cannot be projected backward
claim_or_observation: Medieval Ainnurruvar Nanadesi Manigramam and related institutions are not automatic models for the epic's early historic economy
notes: Binding screenplay production and publicity rule

title: Imported objects do not prove foreign colonies
claim_or_observation: Amphorae coins and imported ceramics prove circulation but not a Roman quarter or foreign ownership of every object
notes: Binding archaeology and publicity rule

title: Coin finds do not prove fully monetised exchange
claim_or_observation: Coins and weights do not establish that every market transaction used standard coin denominations
notes: Binding prop dialogue and market-design rule

title: Economy shown as labour chains
claim_or_observation: Objects and wealth are reconstructed through extraction production transport valuation sale repair and disposal
notes: Requires craft labour and economic-history review

title: Craft technique is not ethnicity
claim_or_observation: A bead pottery metal or textile technique cannot by itself define the worker's ethnicity or social identity
notes: Binding casting costume and publicity rule

title: Poverty follows disrupted work and access
claim_or_observation: Hunger and migration are visualised through injury lost tools failed markets interrupted transport and exclusion from goods
notes: Connects economy and hunger dossiers
